steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: When a battery powers a torch, what happens to the energy?
options: The energy is created by the battery
The energy is transferred from one store to another
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! Energy is never created or destroyed — it is transferred between stores. The battery’s chemical store transfers energy to the thermal and light pathways of the torch. Saying energy is ‘used up’ is scientifically inaccurate.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Energy is transferred from the chemical store of the battery to the surroundings by light and heating. Energy is never created or used up — only transferred.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA GCSE Physics specification 4.1.1 — Energy stores and systems

steps.title: Energy Stores and Transfers
steps.bodyHTML: Energy is stored in 8 energy stores: kinetic, thermal, chemical, gravitational potential, elastic potential, electrostatic, magnetic, and nuclear. Energy is transferred between stores by four pathways: mechanically (by a force), electrically, by heating, or by radiation (light/sound waves). Energy is never created or destroyed — only transferred.
commonErrors: Saying energy is 'used up' or 'lost' — it is transferred (often to thermal stores of surroundings)
Confusing energy stores with energy pathways/transfer methods
Forgetting that sound is a transfer pathway, not a store

steps.title: See it in action
steps.instruction: Drag the sliders to explore how energy transfers between stores. Watch the energy bar chart update in real time.

aqa.questionText: A ball is held above the ground and then dropped. Describe the energy transfers that take place as the ball falls. Ignore air resistance.

steps.label: Identify the starting store
steps.content: The ball is held at a height, so it has energy in its gravitational potential energy store.

steps.label: Identify the transfer
steps.content: As it falls, height decreases so GPE decreases. Speed increases so KE increases.

steps.label: State the transfer
steps.content: Energy is transferred from the gravitational potential energy store to the kinetic energy store.

aqa.finalAnswer: Energy is transferred from the gravitational potential energy store to the kinetic energy store as the ball falls.

edexcel.questionText: A car brakes and comes to a stop. Describe the energy transfer that takes place.

steps.label: Identify the stores
steps.content: Moving car has energy in its kinetic energy store. Braking involves friction.

steps.label: Describe the transfer
steps.content: Energy is transferred from the kinetic energy store of the car to the thermal energy store of the brakes (and surroundings) by heating.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — KE to thermal; 1 mark — by heating/friction

edexcel.finalAnswer: Energy is transferred from the kinetic energy store to the thermal energy store of the brakes by heating.

ocr-gateway.questionText: A catapult is stretched and then released, launching a stone. Describe the energy transfers.

steps.label: Identify starting store
steps.content: The stretched catapult has energy in its elastic potential energy store.

steps.label: Describe the transfer
steps.content: When released, energy is transferred from the elastic potential energy store to the kinetic energy store of the stone.

ocr-gateway.finalAnswer: Elastic potential energy store → kinetic energy store of the stone.

ocr-21c.questionText: A kettle is switched on. Describe the energy transfers that take place.

steps.label: Identify stores
steps.content: Electrical energy is supplied from the mains. The water heats up.

steps.label: Describe
steps.content: Energy is transferred electrically from the mains to the thermal energy store of the water.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — electrical pathway; 1 mark — thermal store of water

ocr-21c.finalAnswer: Energy is transferred electrically to the thermal energy store of the water.

wjec.questionText: A torch is turned on. Name the energy store before and after the transfer.

steps.label: Before
steps.content: Chemical energy store of the battery.

steps.label: After
steps.content: Energy is transferred to the thermal energy store of the surroundings and by radiation (light).

wjec.finalAnswer: Chemical energy store → thermal store of surroundings + light radiation.

aqa.questionText: A wind turbine generates electricity. Which energy store does the energy start in?

options.label: B
options.text: Kinetic energy store of the wind

markSchemeSteps: Energy starts in the kinetic energy store of the moving air (wind)
aqa.markSchemeAward: 1 mark correct store identified · 1 mark mention of wind/air movement
aqa.commonMistake: Students often say 'wind energy' which is not an energy store — the correct answer is kinetic energy store.

edexcel.questionText: A stretched rubber band is released. What energy transfer takes place?
markSchemeSteps: Elastic potential energy store → kinetic energy store
edexcel.markSchemeAward: 1 mark correct stores · 1 mark direction of transfer

ocr-gateway.questionText: Which of these is NOT an energy store?
markSchemeSteps: Heating is a transfer pathway, not a store

ocr-21c.questionText: A person eats food and then runs. Describe the main energy transfers.
markSchemeSteps: Chemical energy store (food) → kinetic energy store (running)
ocr-21c.markSchemeAward: 1 mark chemical store · 1 mark kinetic store

wjec.questionText: Name the energy store in a compressed spring.

rearrangements.formula: Kinetic, Thermal, Chemical, GPE, Elastic PE, Electrostatic, Magnetic, Nuclear
rearrangements.label: All 8 stores

rearrangements.formula: Mechanically, Electrically, By heating, By radiation
rearrangements.label: All 4 pathways

equation.examTip: Always name the specific energy STORE (e.g. ‘kinetic energy store’) — never just say ‘kinetic energy’ without ‘store’. Examiners penalise vague language.

boardNotes.aqa: You must name specific stores and pathways. ‘Light energy’ and ‘sound energy’ are NOT accepted — use ‘by radiation’.
boardNotes.edexcel: Edexcel accepts both ‘energy store’ and ‘energy transfer’ language.
boardNotes.ocr-gateway: OCR Gateway uses the stores model. Learn all 8 stores.
boardNotes.ocr-21c: OCR 21C may refer to ‘energy dissipation’ — this means energy transferred to thermal stores of surroundings.
boardNotes.wjec: WJEC expects clear identification of stores and transfer pathways.
